2606.03601.title: DDOR: Delta Debugging for Explainable Overrefusal Testing and Repair
2606.03601.paper_summary: While safety alignment and guardrails help large language models (LLMs) avoid harmful outputs, they can also induce overrefusal, i.e., unwarranted rejection of benign queries that merely appear risky. We present DDOR (Delta Debugging for OverRefusal), a fully automated and explainable framework for overrefusal testing and repair in a black-box setting, where only model inputs and outputs are accessible and internal safety mechanisms remain opaque. DDOR applies delta debugging to localize minimal refusal-triggering fragments (mRTFs) that provide phrase-level, explainable evidence for why a refusal occurs. Conditioned on these mRTFs, DDOR generates diverse, context-rich prompts and performs multi-oracle validation to filter intrinsically unsafe or ambiguous cases, producing scalable and model-specific overrefusal test suites (approximately 1K cases per model). Beyond evaluation, we further leverage localized mRTFs to perform targeted prompt repair, substantially reducing overrefusal while preserving the original intent and maintaining safety on genuinely harmful inputs. Overall, DDOR offers a practical end-to-end solution to both evaluate and mitigate overrefusal, improving LLM usability without sacrificing safety.
2606.03601.paper_summary_zh: 雖然安全對齊和防護措施幫助大型語言模型（LLMs）避免有害輸出，但它們也可能導致過度拒絕，即對看似風險的良性查詢的不當拒絕。我們提出了DDOR（Delta Debugging for OverRefusal），這是一個完全自動化且可解釋的框架，用於在黑箱環境中進行過度拒絕測試和修復，在這種環境中，只有模型的輸入和輸出是可訪問的，而內部安全機制則保持不透明。DDOR應用增量調試來定位最小拒絕觸發片段（mRTFs），這些片段提供了逐詞級別的可解釋證據，解釋為何會發生拒絕。基於這些mRTFs，DDOR生成多樣化的、豐富上下文的提示，並執行多預言者驗證，以過濾內在不安全或模棱兩可的案例，產生可擴展且特定於模型的過度拒絕測試套件（每個模型約1K個案例）。除了評估之外，我們還進一步利用本地化的mRTFs進行針對性的提示修復，顯著減少過度拒絕，同時保留原始意圖並保持對真正有害輸入的安全性。總體而言，DDOR提供了一個實用的端到端解決方案，既能評估又能減輕過度拒絕，提高LLM的可用性而不犧牲安全性。
2606.03601.author: Qinyan Zhou et.al.
2606.03601.authors: Qinyan Zhou, Peixin Zhang, Jun Sun, Haonan Zhang, Dongxia Wang
